name: penumbra-glspike-00-multi
description: Penumbra native-GLSL spike (Swath 4) regression via VIEWPORT capture (CaptureToBitmap) — no GPU readback, no crash risk. Runs PenumbraGlMulti so the in-process GLSL ray-march conduit renders five atoms in one frame: four separately-compiled TAPES (sphere/box/torus/gyroid, concatenated into one tape texture with per-atom header offsets) + the Benchy mesh-SDF GRID. UNVERIFIED whether CaptureToBitmap includes the conduit's PostDrawObjects GL draw: the LESSONS #2 'overlay not captured' rule is about DrawForeground blitting an EXTERNAL image (the out-of-process studio), but this spike draws REAL GL into Rhino's framebuffer in PostDrawObjects, which the offscreen capture may include. If the candidate is blank/grey, CaptureToBitmap does not see it and the spike needs a dump via a SAFE mechanism (RealtimeDisplayMode at spike-graduation — a raw glGetTexImage readback in the draw callback crashed Rhino natively, reverted). Requires Penumbra.GlSpike.rhp registered for auto-load + the spike artifacts (scene.multi.json + benchy.grid.f32) under packages/host-node/spike. The conduit settles to full quality via its idle pump, so the checkpoint waits before capturing. v1 = capture mode (no baseline); approve the candidate, then switch to pixel-diff.

setup.vlmDescription: A Rhino viewport showing several distinct ray-marched SDF shapes in a row: a magenta sphere, a magenta rounded box, a magenta torus, a magenta gyroid lattice cube, and a blue toy boat (3DBenchy). Solid shaded surfaces, NOT an empty grey grid and NOT bounding boxes.
